learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate an understanding of the thought of various philosophers and theorists working in France, including Beauvoir, Le Doeuff,  Kristeva, Irigaray, Cixous, Duras, Wittig, and Malabou.
Reflect critically on the links between philosophy, literature, and psychoanalytic theory.
Demonstrate effective communication skills, specifically the lucid presentation of ideas, arguments, and debates in written essays and oral presentations.
Demonstrate independent thinking and creative responses to both course readings and lecture material in relation to essays, journal entries, weekly tutorial discussions, and debates.
Demonstrate critical judgment in respect to what is deemed to be proper or legitimate philosophical inquiry.
Reflect critically on existing philosophical and common sense assumptions about womens relation to thought.
Understand the context of dominant philosophical, ethical, and social experiences in post-war France.
Appreciate what constitutes a decidedly French approach to philosophical thought in both historical and cultural terms.

assessments.task_description:
Students will attend tutorials, read Required Readings, and participate in discussion and debate. Tutorials commence in the second week of semester. There are ten tutorials allocated across the semester. Reading for the tutorials is required. These readings, along with the material from the lectures, will be discussed in each tutorial.

AI: This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
Details: A detailed description of what is expected in the Short Essay is available on Blackboard under 'Assessment'. The short essay scheduled during week 6 will cover topics from weeks 1-5 of the course.

Word Limits: A word count that is within ±10% of the set length (750 words) is acceptable. Word count does not include footnotes; however, discursive footnotes are included in the word count. A word count that is outside these 10% will receive a proportionate penalty and will be graded against the grading criteria.

Criteria: Specific marking criteria and/or marking rubrics are available in the 'Assessment' folder on Blackboard.

learning_activities.topic:
Introduction - Feminism, Philosophy, & the Exclusion of Women

Week 1 introduces the relationship between feminism and philosophy as fields of inquiry, and discusses the importance of feminist thinking in everyday life. We consider the historical exclusion of women from the discipline of philosophy, and discuss the positioning of 'rationality' as both white and masculine in philosophical discourse.


Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04, L05, L06
learning_activities.topic_title: Introduction - Feminism, Philosophy, & the Exclusion of Women
learning_activities.topic_description: Introduction - Feminism, Philosophy, & the Exclusion of Women Week 1 introduces the relationship between feminism and philosophy as fields of inquiry, and discusses the importance of feminist thinking in everyday life. We consider the historical exclusion of women from the discipline of philosophy, and discuss the positioning of 'rationality' as both white and masculine in philosophical discourse.

learning_activities.topic:
The Status of 'Woman' - Femininity, Ontology, & the Second Wave

Week 2 examines the existential and material situation of women as analysed in 'second wave' feminist thought and establishes some of the key theoretical ideas of Western feminism in the 20th and 21st centuries from both an Anglo-American and European perspective.


Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04, L05, L06
learning_activities.topic_title: The Status of 'Woman' - Femininity, Ontology, & the Second Wave
learning_activities.topic_description: The Status of 'Woman' - Femininity, Ontology, & the Second Wave Week 2 examines the existential and material situation of women as analysed in 'second wave' feminist thought and establishes some of the key theoretical ideas of Western feminism in the 20th and 21st centuries from both an Anglo-American and European perspective.

learning_activities.topic:
The Status of 'Woman'

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_description: The Status of 'Woman'

learning_activities.topic:
Sex Wars - Sexuality & Violence Against Women

There will be no lecture or tutorials this week, due to the Ekka Public Holiday.


Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05, L06
learning_activities.topic_description: Sex Wars - Sexuality & Violence Against Women There will be no lecture or tutorials this week, due to the Ekka Public Holiday.

learning_activities.topic:
Gendered Embodiment and Dangerous Bodies

Week 4 focuses on Anglo-American considerations of sex and sexuality in the 'second wave,' particularly Western feminist critiques of pornography, masculine sexuality, the sexual objectification of women, rape culture, and violence against women under patriarchy.

Content Warning: This week will engage with discussions of sexual violence.


Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_description: Gendered Embodiment and Dangerous Bodies Week 4 focuses on Anglo-American considerations of sex and sexuality in the 'second wave,' particularly Western feminist critiques of pornography, masculine sexuality, the sexual objectification of women, rape culture, and violence against women under patriarchy. Content Warning: This week will engage with discussions of sexual violence.

learning_activities.topic:
Gendered Embodiment and Dangerous Bodies

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_description: Gendered Embodiment and Dangerous Bodies

learning_activities.topic:
Differences that Matter - Critiques of White Feminisms

Week 5 explores Black and Australian Indigenous critiques of Western feminist thought concerning intersections of race and gender, the racism of white feminists, and the unexamined 'whiteness' of Western feminism more broadly.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_title: Differences that Matter - Critiques of White Feminisms
learning_activities.topic_description: Differences that Matter - Critiques of White Feminisms Week 5 explores Black and Australian Indigenous critiques of Western feminist thought concerning intersections of race and gender, the racism of white feminists, and the unexamined 'whiteness' of Western feminism more broadly.

learning_activities.topic:
Differences that Matter

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_description: Differences that Matter

learning_activities.topic:
Becoming Woman - Pyschoanalysis, Language, & Subjectivity

Week 6 turns to psychoanalytic theory as a key philosophical framework for later feminist thought and a significant account of gendered socialisation, discourse, and subjectivity under patriarchy.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_description: Becoming Woman - Pyschoanalysis, Language, & Subjectivity Week 6 turns to psychoanalytic theory as a key philosophical framework for later feminist thought and a significant account of gendered socialisation, discourse, and subjectivity under patriarchy.

learning_activities.topic:
Becoming Woman

First in-class short essay to be completed during this tutorial.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_description: Becoming Woman First in-class short essay to be completed during this tutorial.

learning_activities.topic:
Revolt, She Said - Marginality in Western Culture

Week 7 discusses the cultural position of femininity and the mother in Western patriarchy, the possibilities of art and language as both liberating and repressive cultural spaces, and the notion of feminist politics as a form of revolt.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_title: Revolt, She Said - Marginality in Western Culture
learning_activities.topic_description: Revolt, She Said - Marginality in Western Culture Week 7 discusses the cultural position of femininity and the mother in Western patriarchy, the possibilities of art and language as both liberating and repressive cultural spaces, and the notion of feminist politics as a form of revolt.

learning_activities.topic:
Revolt, She Said

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L06, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_description: Revolt, She Said

learning_activities.topic:
Sexual Differences & the Critique of Phallocentrism

Week 8 examines Western patriarchal culture's relation to 'the body' through the concept of the abject and it's cultural relation to femininity. Abjection is further explored through closer case studies of ableism and fatphobia in Western culture and the connection of these forms of oppression with patriarchy.

Content Warning: This week will engage with discussions of body shaming and eating disorders.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_description: Sexual Differences & the Critique of Phallocentrism Week 8 examines Western patriarchal culture's relation to 'the body' through the concept of the abject and it's cultural relation to femininity. Abjection is further explored through closer case studies of ableism and fatphobia in Western culture and the connection of these forms of oppression with patriarchy. Content Warning: This week will engage with discussions of body shaming and eating disorders.

learning_activities.topic:
Sexual Differences

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_description: Sexual Differences

learning_activities.topic:
Talking Back - Women's Writing, Speech, & Anger

Week 9 turns to look at the concept of 'sexual difference' as an alternative framing of the relation between masculine and feminine in Western culture, critiques of psychoanalysis and philosophy as phallocentric discourses, and the feminist strategy of 'mimesis'. These ideas will be further explored through their relevance and application in Indigenous Australian feminist philosophy.

Content Warning: This week contains mentions of the stolen generations and colonial violence in Australia.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_description: Talking Back - Women's Writing, Speech, & Anger Week 9 turns to look at the concept of 'sexual difference' as an alternative framing of the relation between masculine and feminine in Western culture, critiques of psychoanalysis and philosophy as phallocentric discourses, and the feminist strategy of 'mimesis'. These ideas will be further explored through their relevance and application in Indigenous Australian feminist philosophy. Content Warning: This week contains mentions of the stolen generations and colonial violence in Australia.

learning_activities.topic_description: Talking Back

learning_activities.topic:
In the Borderlands - Identity, Intersections, & the Postcolonial 'Woman'

Week 10 discusses feminist theories on the exchange of women, sociality between women under patriarchy, and the significance of maternal genealogy, particularly for Women of Colour and Indigenous women.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_title: In the Borderlands - Identity, Intersections, & the Postcolonial 'Woman'
learning_activities.topic_description: In the Borderlands - Identity, Intersections, & the Postcolonial 'Woman' Week 10 discusses feminist theories on the exchange of women, sociality between women under patriarchy, and the significance of maternal genealogy, particularly for Women of Colour and Indigenous women.

learning_activities.topic:
In the Borderlands

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_description: In the Borderlands

learning_activities.topic:
When the Goods Get Together - The Exchange of Women & Sociality Amongst Women

Week 11 examines the feminist political potential of women's speech and writing, the philosophical and discursive economy of phallocentric culture, and the importance of anger and emotion in the expression of a feminist voice.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_title: When the Goods Get Together - The Exchange of Women & Sociality Amongst Women

learning_activities.topic:
When the Goods Get Together

Second in-class short essay to be completed during this tutorial.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_description: When the Goods Get Together Second in-class short essay to be completed during this tutorial.

learning_activities.topic:
The Mark of Gender - Heterosexism & Thinking Beyond 'Woman'

Week 12 builds on previous discussions of race and colonialism to critique Western feminism from a postcolonial context. In particular, we explore how race and indigeneity differently position women and gender-diverse people in their relationships to gender, language, and identity.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
learning_activities.topic_title: The Mark of Gender - Heterosexism & Thinking Beyond 'Woman'
learning_activities.topic_description: The Mark of Gender - Heterosexism & Thinking Beyond 'Woman' Week 12 builds on previous discussions of race and colonialism to critique Western feminism from a postcolonial context. In particular, we explore how race and indigeneity differently position women and gender-diverse people in their relationships to gender, language, and identity.

learning_activities.topic:
The Mark of Gender

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_description: The Mark of Gender

learning_activities.topic:
I Thought the Future Would be Cooler: Feminist Subjects & the Non-Binary

Week 13 examines critiques of heterosexism and critical analyses of sex/gender as a category of thought. We discuss the relationships between categories of gender and sexuality, and of heterosexuality with processes of colonisation.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
learning_activities.topic_title: I Thought the Future Would be Cooler: Feminist Subjects & the Non-Binary

We discuss the relationships between categories of gender and sexuality, and of heterosexuality with processes of colonisation.
